topic: Applied Psychology Options (Child / Criminal / Environmental / Sport and Exercise)
subject: psychology

items.q: According to differential association theory, how is criminal behaviour learned?
items.correct: Through interaction with others, learning attitudes and techniques favourable to crime.
items.misconception: It is inherited entirely through genes from criminal parents.
items.random_wrong: It is caused only by poverty with no learning involved.
items.why: Students confuse a social-learning explanation with a purely genetic or economic one.

items.q: Is offender profiling an exact, reliable science?
items.correct: No; profiling can assist investigations but is probabilistic and has produced notable errors.
items.misconception: Profiling identifies the exact offender with certainty.
items.random_wrong: Profiling is only used after a suspect has confessed.
items.why: Media portrayals lead students to overestimate the precision of profiling.

items.q: Does Eysenck's theory claim criminals simply choose to offend freely?
items.correct: No; it links offending to inherited personality dimensions such as high extraversion and neuroticism affecting conditioning.
items.misconception: Eysenck argued crime is purely a free, rational choice.
items.random_wrong: Eysenck's theory is about memory rather than personality.
items.why: Students overlook the biological-personality basis of Eysenck's explanation.

items.q: Top-down offender profiling was developed by:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: FBI. “Canter” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Differential association theory was proposed by:
items.why: Correct answer: Sutherland. “Eysenck” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Lombroso publish his atavistic form theory?
items.why: The real figure is 1876 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
